business.headline: US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

Steady adjustment of rupee is okay, a free fall is not: Raghuram RajanIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, former Reserve Bank of India Governor Raghuram Rajan shares his thoughts on how to deal with the ongoing conflict in West Asia's impact on the global and Indian economies and more.

Something is off: Raghuram Rajan questions India's growth narrative"Something is off" with India's economy, former RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan said, questioning whether official GDP figures showing growth of more than 7 per cent accurately reflect conditions on the ground.

India to stay world's fastest-growing major economy, growth at 6.6%: World BankIndia will remain the world's fastest-growing major economy, with growth expected to slow from 7.7% in FY2025-26 to 6.6% in FY2026-27 before recovering to 7.2% the following year.

Why alcohol sales are set to slow globally over next 10 years but grow in IndiaAs global alcohol consumption enters a phase of slowdown, India appears to be charting a very different growth path. What lies behind this contrasting trend?

SpaceX IPO could create 4,400 employee millionaires. Here's howSpaceX's planned IPO at $135 a share could make more than 4,400 current and former employees millionaires. The public listing could also widen retail participation and trigger fresh spending by long-time equity holders.

Markets end lower as IT slump and US inflation fears weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex fell 150.63 points, or 0.20%, to close at 73,832.55, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 53.35 points, or 0.23%, to settle at 23,161.60.

India overtakes the world's biggest economy in construction growthThe country has been investing heavily in highways, railways, airports, ports, metro systems and logistics networks. These projects are not only creating jobs and boosting economic activity today but are also laying the foundation for future growth.

Is your CIBIL score below 730? RBI's new rules may make loans harder to getThe RBI's Expected Credit Loss rules from April 1, 2027 could make banks stricter with borrowers below a 730 CIBIL score. The shift may raise rates, collateral demands and rejection risks for applicants with thin or irregular credit histories.

8th Pay Commission: Will state government employees get salary hikes too?The 8th Central Pay Commission has been constituted by the Union government to review salaries, pensions, allowances and other service conditions of Central government employees and pensioners.

From PAN, TDS to tax regime: 7 Form 16 checks you can't miss before filing ITRFor many salaried employees, Form 16 is the starting point of ITR filing. But before you file your return, have you checked whether your PAN, TDS credits and tax regime details are actually correct?

Gold and silver are falling from their peaks. 3 things investors must do nowThe shine appears to be fading, at least for now. After soaring to all-time highs earlier this year, gold and silver prices have come under pressure, leaving investors asking a familiar question: is it time to buy more, hold on, or head for the exit?

No E30 at pumps yet, so why has India exempted higher ethanol petrol from tax?India has exempted E22, E25, E27 and E30 petrol blends from excise duty despite no retail rollout yet. The move sets up a policy framework for higher ethanol fuels as questions persist over vehicle compatibility and costs.

Infosys, HCL Tech, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling today?The Nifty IT index fell 2.67% to 27,525.80, emerging as the worst-performing sector on Dalal Street as of 9:50 am. The decline also weighed heavily on the Sensex, where IT majors were among the biggest losers.

Sensex, Nifty fall amid West Asia tensions; IT stocks tumble over 2%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 263.34 points, or 0.36%, to 73,719.84 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 70.70 points, or 0.30%, to 23,144.25 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early indicators pointed to a weak start for domestic equities. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,069 at around 7:43 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,214.95.

OpenDoor lays off entire India team of 250 in AI-driven overhaulOpenDoor is winding down its India operations and moving nearly 250 roles to the United States. The shift to smaller AI-native teams has sparked fresh questions about AI's impact on offshoring.

India scraps excise duty on petrol with 22% to 30% ethanol blendIndia has exempted petrol containing 22% to 30% ethanol from excise duty. The move is aimed at encouraging higher ethanol use and reducing reliance on imported crude oil.

Who borrows the most in India today: Gen Z, millennials or older Indians?India's love affair with credit is growing. As digital lending apps and easy EMIs make borrowing more accessible than ever, one question stands out: who is borrowing the most? Is it young Gen Z, middle-aged millennials, or older Indians?

Sebi proposes new salary disclosure rules for asset managersIn a consultation paper released on Wednesday, Sebi suggested that AMCs should be allowed to disclose the combined remuneration paid to executive-level employees along with the total number of such employees.

entertainment.headline: Who plays Ranga and Billa in Raakh? Meet the faces behind the horror Raakh revisits one of India's most chilling criminal cases, with two actors stepping into roles inspired by the men who shocked the nation in 1978. Here's everything you need to know about the faces behind the series' most disturbing characters.

Geeta Kapur on what reel culture gets right and wrong about Bollywood dance Geeta Kapur shared her thoughts on the rise of reel culture, saying social media has amplified dance trends but cannot replace the lasting impact of Bollywood's iconic hook steps.

Steven Spielberg says Disclosure Day explores misinformation, the search for truth Steven Spielberg said that Disclosure Day was not just an alien-contact film but also a reflection on misinformation, truth and humanity's response to world-changing discoveries.
Shabana Azmi to play antagonist in Emraan Hashmi-Disha Patani's Awarapan 2 Shabana Azmi has joined Emraan Hashmi-Disha Patani's Awarapan 2 as Nafisa, a negative character in the upcoming film.

Women's panel summons Pranit More, Himanshu Jangra over Rs 370 biryani row The National Commission for Women has summoned comedian Pranit More and Himanshu Jangra over remarks made during a Gurugram stand-up show that allegedly glorified sexual coercion.

4 new movies and shows releasing this week on Prime Video, ZEE5 From Suriya's blockbuster action drama Karuppu to the suspense-filled crime thriller Raakh, this week's OTT slate offers something for every viewer. Kangana Ranaut clarifies 'very British' nurse uniform remark: Reforms were made Kangana Ranaut clarified that her comments on nurses' uniforms reflected a personal view and acknowledged that reforms had already been implemented years ago. The actor spoke about the issue while promoting Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, in which she plays a nurse.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day gets early release in India Peter Parker's next big-screen outing is inching closer, and Indian fans are getting a small head start. Sony Pictures has rolled out a fresh update for Spider-Man: Brand New Day as anticipation around the film continues to mount.

My queen Madhuri Dixit is a scene-stealer: Priyanka Chopra reviews Maa Behen Priyanka Chopra Jonas praised Suresh Triveni's Maa Behen for its clever storytelling and powerful performances, especially highlighting Madhuri Dixit's captivating presence.

5 new movies and shows releasing this week on Netflix, JioHotstar Netflix and JioHotstar are rolling out five titles across genres this week. The line-up ranges from horror-comedy and crime thrillers to true crime and international drama.

Was Madhuri Dixit's blouse a fight against patriarchy? Maa Behen director answers Suresh Triveni addressed the debate over the sleeveless blouse worn by Madhuri Dixit's character in Maa Behen. He said the garment reflects personal choice within the story, even as viewers read it as a symbol of patriarchy.

Jana Nayagan leak case: Absconding mastermind arrested in Chennai The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested the alleged mastermind in the Jana Nayagan footage leak case. The latest arrests have taken the total to 19 as the probe and piracy warning continue.

Rs 1.7 crore for Welcome To The Jungle? Akshay Kumar reacts to fee rumours At the trailer launch of Welcome to the Jungle, Akshay Kumar dismissed reports that he charged Rs 1.7 crore for the film. He said the project matters to him because Neeraj Vora wrote the story.

Hrithik Roshan replaces SRK in Jailer 2, reunites with Rajinikanth after 40 years Hrithik Roshan has joined Rajinikanth's Jailer 2 for a special cameo, with his scenes set to be shot in Chennai later this month. The appearance reunites them on screen 40 years after Bhagwaan Dada and adds to the sequel's star-heavy line-up.

Main Vaapas Aaunga review: Diljit Dosanjh leads Imtiaz Ali's weakest filmRelease Date:Thu Jun 11, 2026 Main Vaapas Aaunga movie review: Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga follows a grandson decoding his dying grandfather's Partition memories. The film reaches for grief and belonging, but its pacing and politics blunt the impact.

CM Vijay's Mersal to re-release in theatres as actor-politician's pre-birthday gift Director Atlee's Mersal will return to theatres in Chennai and Chengalpattu on June 19 ahead of Vijay's birthday. The re-release gives fans a big-screen celebration as Jana Nayagan's release remains unclear.

general news.headline: Trump says Iran deal could be signed in Europe this weekend, JD Vance to attendDonald Trump said that a signing ceremony could take place in Europe as early as this weekend, although he would not attend personally. Vice President JD Vance is expected to represent the United States at the event.

Mexico vs South Africa Live: Jimenez, Quinones give hosts 2-0 win in openerMexico vs South Africa Live Updates: Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez have fired co-hosts Mexico to an important 2-0 win over South Africa in the FIFA World Cup 2026 opener. The match saw 3 red-cards, with South Africa's Yaya Sithole and Thema Zwane along with Mexico's Ceser Montes getting sent off with straight reds.

Trump cancels planned attack on Iran, claims US-Iran deal approved by alliesDonald Trump said that he had called off the planned attacks because negotiations had moved forward rapidly and received approval from senior Iranian leaders.

Blockade impartial: US rains Hellfire missiles on Jalveer, 20 Indians rescuedAmerican forces on Thursday hit oil tanker M/T Jalveer, which was being crewed by 20 Indian sailors, with two Hellfire missiles. All 20 have been rescued. This was the third attack in four days on a ship being crewed by Indians in the Gulf of Oman. The US said it was enforcing the blockade on Iran impartially.

Pakistan launches 6 satellites to watch India, Isro's NavIC still in crisisPakistan has launched six Chinese-backed Earth-observation satellites as India struggles to restore NavIC. The contrast has sharpened concern over surveillance capability and the reliability of India's sovereign navigation network.

Form human shield: Islamist Jamaat to march to India border over 'push-ins'Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and allied Islamist parties have announced that they would march to various border points with India on Friday protesting against the alleged Indian push-ins and killings near the frontier. The anti-Hasina student's NCP has called for Bangladeshis to form a human shield along the border even as high-level talks between India and Bangladesh are being held in New Delhi.

I gave up my H-1B visa: Ex-Microsoft techie explains why he returned to IndiaFrom winning the H-1B lottery on his first attempt to working at Microsoft, Hemant Shetty lived the life countless Indian professionals dream of. However, Shetty returned to India. The restrictions on entrepreneurship and uncertainties linked to the H-1B visa norms made him choose India over the US.

World Cup referee headset sparks RoboCop memes during Mexico openerFIFA World Cup 2026: The football was barely minutes old when the referees stole the spotlight. The World Cup fans were left doing a double take after spotting Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio wearing a futuristic headset that quickly sparked Xbox and RoboCop comparisons online.

Rare full house at NITI Aayog as all 28 CMs join PM Modi-led meetingAll 28 chief ministers attended the 11th NITI Aayog Governing Council meeting chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, marking a sharp departure from the boycotts and absences that had characterised previous editions.

Delhi-NCR hit by heavy rain, strong winds; more showers expected FridayHeavy rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds swept across Delhi-NCR on Thursday evening after days of intense heat. The IMD has warned of more rain and severe weather across North India on Friday.

10 rounds in 5 seconds: Bishnoi gang shoots gym owner dead, warns of more attacksA gym operator was shot dead by motorcycle-borne attackers near Fawwara Chowk in Hansi during an early morning exercise session. A young woman was injured by bullet fragments as police and forensic teams began an investigation after CCTV footage surfaced.

Cyber cell registers case against Pranit More, Himanshu Jangra in Rs 370 biryani rowThe controversy began after Himanshu Jangra recounted, during the show, a date when he spent Rs 370 on a plate of chicken biryani. When the woman asked him to drop her home, Jangra said he sought sexual favours for the money he spent on the biryani.

Thunderstorms, hailstorms and heavy rain across large parts of India on FridayThe IMD has issued widespread weather warnings for Friday as the monsoon advances and multiple systems intensify conditions. The alerts signal severe rain, thunderstorms, hail and parallel heatwave conditions across several regions.

Live: Iran yet to approve US deal, but odds of approval rising, state media saysUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The apparent diplomatic breakthrough announced by US President Donald Trump was quickly clouded by uncertainty after Iranian media pushed back against his claims that key points of an agreement had already been approved. Trump said he cancelled planned strikes on Iran because discussions had reached the highest levels of the Iranian leadership and had secured broad regional backing. However, Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency later cited a source close to the negotiating team as saying that Tehran had not approved any text for a preliminary memorandum of understanding with Washington. The conflicting accounts left the true state of negotiations unclear, even as both sides continued indirect contacts. Trump earlier vowed to take Kharg Island, which handles roughly 90% of Iran's crude exports. Plans to capture the island have been drawn up for months but continuously shelved because it was considered too risky. Iranian officials have maintained that the country is prepared for any escalation and have rejected suggestions that military pressure would force concessions. Meanwhile, reports from Iranian and Western officials indicate that negotiations remain active, with major issues such as sanctions relief, frozen Iranian assets and regional security arrangements still unresolved. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

Grabbed my hair, slapped me: Cop arrested for assaulting ex-girlfriend, familyA Delhi Police constable has been arrested after his former girlfriend accused him of assaulting her, threatening her family, and attempting to force his way into her Gurugram residence. The complaint also alleges a previous assault and blackmail through threats to leak a private video, while CCTV footage of the latest incident has surfaced online.

11 kids among 13 killed in airstrike in Afghanistan. UN doesn't name culprit PakPakistani airstrikes in eastern Afghanistan killed 13 civilians, including 11 children, said the Taliban. The UN, which said it documented the casualties, faced criticism after its statement as it did not name the country behind the strikes.

Attacks must end: India condemns US strike on vessel near Oman that killed 3 IndiansThe government said it had summoned the US Chargé d'affaires on Wednesday to formally protest a strike on a ship off the coast of Oman that killed 3 Indian nationals. Three ships carrying Indian crew members have been hit by the US Navy in the last four days, with one struck by US Hellfire missiles on Thursday.

Apple is making it almost impossible for thieves to resell your stolen iPhoneApple's latest security upgrades are making stolen iPhones much harder to reset and resell, with police claiming the move is already reducing criminals' ability to profit from phone theft. The company has also started sharing data with law enforcement to better track what happens to stolen devices.

Pak Army guns down 16 protesters in POK's Rawalakot as demonstrations intensifyAt least 16 protesters were shot dead by Pakistani security forces after they opened fire on demonstrators in Rawalakot in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir (POK) amid widening protests across the region.

Real Madrid announce Jose Mourinho as new manager to headline comeback era Real Madrid have turned to a familiar face in their search for a revival, bringing Jose Mourinho back to the Santiago Bernabeu on a three-year deal. More than a decade after his first spell, "The Special One" is now tasked with leading another rebuild.

Mustafizur, Taskin star as Bangladesh secure maiden ODI series win vs Australia Bangladesh beat Australia by five wickets in Dhaka to seal their first ODI series win over them. The result was sealed by a sensational early burst from Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed with the new ball that wrecked the Aussie top order.

The match saw 3 red-cards, with South Africa's Yaya Sithole and Thema Zwane along with Mexico's Ceser Montes get sent off with straight reds.

Germany's class act: Players fund 600 fans' trip amid transport price hike Germany's players will cover the costs for 600 supporters to travel to their group match against Ecuador in New Jersey. The gesture was done after the travel costs in the US were spiked for the FIFA World Cup.

Barred from World Cup, Somalian referee to officiate major European fixture Omar Abdulkadir Artan has been appointed to referee the UEFA Super Cup in Salzburg on August 12. The call comes weeks after the Somalian official missed the World Cup despite holding a valid US visa.

Manu Bhaker to spearhead India's shooting challenge at Asian Games The National Rifle Association of India announced a 30-member shooting contingent for the 2026 Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya. The squad consists of as many as four double starters, including Manu Bhaker, who will compete in multiple events.

No rush on Stokes' future as England consider alcohol ban after nightclub row Rob Key is considering tougher restrictions, including a possible alcohol ban, after Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson were dropped following a London nightclub incident. Key offered no guarantees when it comes to Ben Stokes continuing as Test captain as the investigation continues.

After lucky escape vs Sri Lanka, India A lose to Afghanistan in Dambulla tri-series Talent tri-series, IND A vs AFG A: Afghanistan A mounted a fearless chase after rain revised India A's 349 into a 38-over target in Dambulla. Imran Mir and Bahir Shah stayed ahead of the DLS par before another downpour sealed the result on Thursday.

Men's ODI World Cup 2027 set to run from October 4 to November 21 The ICC has broadly approved an October 4 to November 21 window for the 2027 Men's ODI World Cup across South Africa, Zimbabwe and Namibia. The event restores a 14-team format, returns the men's World Cup to Africa, and sits alongside wider decisions on the next FTP and WTC structure.

Grand Slam pay war: Champions to earn Rs 46 crore as Wimbledon unveils record purseWimbledon has raised its 2026 prize purse to a record £64.2 million, with singles champions set to earn £3.6 million each. The increase follows player protests over Grand Slam revenue sharing and lifts payouts across the draw.

Sooryavanshi trades aggression for restraint in 44-run cameo vs Afghanistan AVaibhav Sooryavanshi showcased a different facet of his batting during India A's clash against Afghanistan A, scoring a brisk 44 off 22 balls while relying more on timing and placement than outright power. The 15-year-old struck nine boundaries and gave India A a flying start.

Another Dravid in India colours: BCCI picks Rahul's son Anvay in U19 squadRahul Dravid's younger son, Anvay, has been named in India's Under-19 one-day squad for Sri Lanka. The call-up rewards his domestic form and begins his pathway in the next World Cup cycle.

All you need to know about FIFA World Cup opening ceremonyThe FIFA World Cup opens with three ceremonies across Mexico City, Toronto and Los Angeles over two days. Shakira will headline the first opening ceremony in Mexico on Thursday, performing her official tournament single "Dai Dai" live for the first time.

ICC annual rankings update: India remain the world's top ODI sideIndia retained the No. 1 spot in the latest ICC men's ODI team rankings following the annual update, although their advantage at the top was reduced. India now lead second-placed New Zealand by five rating points, while Australia remain third.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group K preview: Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal in a pickleGroup K offers a fascinating mix of established contenders and ambitious challengers. PV Sindhu's husband becomes an accidental coach. She has plenty to Sai about itFeeling slightly off, PV Sindhu asked husband Venkata Datta Sai to sit in her corner during her second-round match against Isharani Baruah in Sydney. She then beat her compatriot to reach the Australian Open quarter-finals on Thursday.

Australia add India-born Nikhil Chaudhary to T20Is squad for Bangladesh seriesDelhi-born all-rounder Nikhil Chaudhary could become the first Indian-born man to represent Australia in international cricket in more than 60 years after earning a call-up to the country's T20I squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

Hitman in range: Rohit Sharma gets into groove for India vs Afghanistan ODIsRohit Sharma returned to India's white-ball nets and gradually found his range after a lay-off. The session eased immediate worries and showed a senior batter measuring himself against his own standards.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.
